prompt: Explain angular momentum clearly and correctly in one coherent answer. Keep it under 180 words and end after the answer.
completion_text:
Angular momentum is the rotational equivalent of linear momentum for objects rotating around a central axis, analogous to how an object’s normal (inertial) mass creates resistance to acceleration under applied force. Angular momentum can be defined as "the quantity of rotation" or more precisely: it measures how much “rotational motion” something has; specifically it's measured in units kilogram‑meter²/second and equals the product of moment of inertia (I) times angular velocity (ω). In symbols L = I · ω.

      - Resistential
         - Resistional (resistance to change), same as the direction of its axis through time derivative dθ/dt).
     So angular
